---
title: Iterators and closures
description: Build lazy iterator pipelines and consume them safely.
---

## Iterators and closures

[Open in Playground](https://playground.xelis.io/?%%2BPSBtaW5fc2NvcmUgfSkKfQ%3D%3D)

Iterators are lazy until consumed by methods such as `collect`, `sum`, or `find`.

```rust filename="iterators.slx" copy
struct TopScoresRpcEvent {
    selected: u64[],
    total: u64
}

entry top_scores(scores: u64[], min_score: u64, limit: u32) -> u64[] {
    let selected: u64[] = scores
        .iter()
        .filter(|score: u64| { return score >= min_score })
        .map(|score: u64| { return score * 10 })
        .take(limit)
        .collect()

    let total: u64 = selected.iter().sum()
    fire_rpc_event(10, TopScoresRpcEvent { selected, total })
    return selected
}

entry first_match(scores: u64[], min_score: u64) -> optional<u64> {
    return scores.iter().find(|score: u64| { return score >= min_score })
}
```

Use `take` or `find` to bound work and avoid scanning more values than needed.
